vlm_prompt:
Describe the data visualization in detail, focusing on the trends, relationships, and key data points. Provide a comprehensive textual description suitable for a screen reader user with advanced data analysis skills. Include axis labels, data ranges, and any significant anomalies. Explain the chart type. Do not use any markdown formatting, asterisks, or bullet points. Write the entire description in plain English text only.

MANDATORY: You MUST write the entire image description in English ONLY.
raw_description:
This is a flowchart illustrating a troubleshooting process for a non-functioning lamp. The chart begins with a rectangular box labeled “Lamp doesn’t work” at the top center. An arrow descends from this box to a diamond-shaped decision box asking “Lamp plugged in?”. A “No” branch extends to the right, leading to a rectangular box labeled “Plug in lamp”. A “Yes” branch descends from the initial decision box to another diamond-shaped decision box asking “Bulb burned out?”. A “Yes” branch extends to the right, leading to a rectangular box labeled “Replace bulb”. A “No” branch descends from this second decision box to a final rectangular box labeled “Repair lamp”. 

The flowchart visually represents a sequential decision-making process. There are no numerical axes or data ranges present, as the chart focuses on logical steps rather than quantitative data. The chart’s structure is entirely hierarchical, with each decision point branching into two possible outcomes. There are no apparent anomalies or unusual patterns, as the chart simply outlines a standard troubleshooting procedure. The flowchart uses a consistent color scheme: yellow for decision boxes, green for action boxes, and white for the background. The arrows indicate the flow of the troubleshooting process, guiding the user through the steps to resolve the issue.
